recent_trades.summary: Adding a 1% starter position in COP on a major LNG supply deal and strong cash flow outlook, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: The 30-year natural gas supply agreement with Alaska LNG solidifies COP's revenue stream and underscores its role in the global energy transition. Sentiment is positive (0.32), and the stock is trading near its 30-day low, offering a discounted entry. This complements existing energy holdings with a mid-cap growth tilt.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 2% position in ABBV on positive regulatory news and valuation opportunity after recent underperformance, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: ABBV received a positive CHMP opinion for MAVIRET in acute hepatitis C, adding to its robust pipeline. The stock has underperformed with a -0.69% 30D change and is near 30-day lows, suggesting the news is not fully priced in. With a 0.30 sentiment score and its defensive healthcare nature, ABBV offers a compelling entry.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming QCOM to 2.5% to capture a 15.8% gain and reallocate to new high-conviction ideas, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: QCOM has delivered strong returns but lacks recent positive catalysts and faces potential rotation out of semiconductors. Reducing weight to 2.5% still maintains exposure while freeing capital for ABBV and COP.

recent_trades.summary: Selling entire AMD position after a 20% gain to lock in profits amid mixed AI chip sentiment and rotation into new catalysts, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: AMD has rallied ~20% from our cost basis, and while chip sentiment remains positive, the news flow today is focused on other AI players like Dell and Cerebras. With the broader market cautious on consumer data, taking profits on this high-beta name and redeploying into undervalued healthcare and energy provides better risk-reward.

recent_trades.summary: Adding CAT on Evercore's upgrade to $1,103 price target and narrative shift toward power infrastructure, with stock at 30-day highs.
recent_trades.reasoning: Evercore just turned more bullish with a $1,103 PT implying significant upside from current $890. CAT is at its 30-day high, up +0.94% over 30 days showing strong momentum. The power infrastructure angle adds a new secular growth catalyst beyond traditional construction cycles. Sentiment is positive (+0.11). Targeting 7% weight using freed-up capital from COST and ORCL exits.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting ORCL as the position is small, slightly underwater (-0.24%), and technicals show a 30-day downtrend from 197 to 192.
recent_trades.reasoning: ORCL was initiated recently but has continued to drift lower. The stock is in a 30-day downtrend (-1.15%) and while sentiment is mildly positive (+0.15), the CoreWeave vs Oracle article suggests competitive pressure. At 3.85% weight this capital can be better deployed.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting COST as it's down -3.38% from cost, at only ~2% weight with neutral sentiment, and underperforming the broader portfolio.
recent_trades.reasoning: COST is the only position with a meaningful unrealized loss (-3.38%) and carries neutral sentiment (+0.00). At just 2% weight it's too small to move the needle and the stock is in a 30-day downtrend from 1045 to 1030. Freeing this capital for higher-conviction names.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing ISRG on +0.22 sentiment, Da Vinci 5 upgrades and multiple analyst watchlist mentions.
recent_trades.reasoning: ISRG exhibits constructive green candles and upward bias; positive moat-deepening news supports adding from current 2.1% to 5% as a healthcare growth diversifier.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to PM on +0.33 positive sentiment and high-yield appeal versus weaker consumer staples peers.
recent_trades.reasoning: PM has stable chart near highs with positive headlines on dividend superiority; current 1.5% weight is under-allocated given sentiment edge, targeting modest increase without exceeding position limits.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming INTC from 7.65% to 4% on -0.16 sentiment and lagging semiconductor momentum versus AMD and QCOM.
recent_trades.reasoning: INTC shows mixed red/green candles and negative news flow around AI competition; reducing exposure to reallocate to higher-conviction names with better technicals and sentiment.

recent_trades.summary: Doubling BlackRock to a 10% weight to capture its strong technical uptrend and highly positive sentiment around tokenization initiatives.
recent_trades.reasoning: BlackRock is exhibiting excellent relative strength, pushing toward its 30-day highs while generating very positive sentiment scores (+0.50). Favorable media coverage regarding its strategic positioning in a $4 trillion tokenization market and reliable dividend growth make it an attractive overweight.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting Oracle completely to free up capital as the stock struggles with downward technical momentum and lacks immediate bullish catalysts.
recent_trades.reasoning: Oracle has been drifting lower with a -1.15% return over the last 30 days and remains below its 20-period moving average. With no fresh, compelling news to support a reversal, capital is better deployed elsewhere.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing EOG from a small weight on very positive free-cash-flow and upgrade news with room to grow the position, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: EOG carries the strongest news tone in this set at +0.51, backed by record free cash flow, shareholder payout support, and a recent Strong Buy upgrade. The stock is stable near the top half of its 30-day range, and at just 1.50% weight it remains under-allocated versus conviction.

recent_trades.summary: Adding modestly to TJX as its earnings beat and raised guidance keep supporting one of the cleaner breakout setups in the portfolio, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: TJX has the strongest combination here of positive sentiment (+0.26), fresh fundamental news, and constructive price action holding near the upper end of its 30-day range. We already added earlier, so this is only a small scale-up from 6.61% to 8.00% rather than an aggressive chase.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting the remaining META stake as legal-risk headlines continue to overhang the stock and the chart is drifting lower intraday, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: META sentiment remains negative at -0.19 and the lawsuit-settlement headline adds asymmetric downside risk without a clear offsetting catalyst. The position is only 1.00% of the portfolio, so a full exit simplifies the book and frees a small amount of capital from a low-conviction holding.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 1.5% position in WMT on post-earnings dip and resilient growth outlook, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Walmart sold off after its recent earnings report, but analysts at RBC highlight resilient underlying growth masked by exogenous factors. The dip provides an entry point into a defensive stock with strong fundamentals, especially as uncertainty persists in other sectors. The 1.5% initial position adds ballast to the portfolio and diversifies away from technology and energy.

recent_trades.summary: Reducing INTC to 1.5% amid negative sentiment and lagging performance in the chip sector, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Intel has negative news sentiment (-0.16) and is underperforming the broader semiconductor group over the past week. While the position holds a small unrealized gain, the lack of near-term positive catalysts and the chip sector's rotation into faster-growing AI names motivate a trim. This sale frees cash for more offensive or defensive plays.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming V to 1.5% on increasing regulatory headwinds in Europe and Brazil, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Visa faces growing regulatory risks in both European and Brazilian payment markets, as flagged by recent headlines. With sentiment turning negative and the stock trading near the top of its 30-day range, I am taking a partial profit (unrealized gain of +3.99%) and reducing exposure to 1.5%. The freed capital will be reallocated to higher-conviction opportunities.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing ORCL on +0.15 sentiment, favorable AI infrastructure comparisons versus CoreWeave and expected Salesforce earnings tailwind, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: ORCL down over 1% intraday offers an attractive entry; boosting from 4.45% weight on AI-cloud momentum that technicals have not fully reflected.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming V on -0.22 sentiment from UK/Europe regulatory worries and broader mega-IPO bubble concerns, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Visa has lagged peers intraday with no offsetting positive catalysts; reducing ~2.3% weight further to redeploy capital into higher-conviction names while respecting the no-repeat rule on unchanged tickers.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to TJX after Q1 earnings beat with raised full-year guidance and strong market share gains signal, upgrading from 6.98% to ~9% weight.
recent_trades.reasoning: TJX delivered a Q1 beat and raised full-year guidance today with sentiment at +0.26. The company showed broad-based strength and international expansion momentum. Current position is at near-cost basis (-0.14%), providing a good entry point to add into this fundamental catalyst — the raised guidance is a new driver not present in my last trade.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming GOOGL further from 7.96% to ~6% given persistent negative sentiment (-0.19) and no clear positive catalyst in today's headlines.
recent_trades.reasoning: GOOGL headlines today are negative (-0.19 sentiment) with no direct positive catalysts. The stock has +15% unrealized gain and I've already trimmed once. Reducing further to free up cash for higher-conviction ideas like TJX and CAT while locking in more gains near the 30-day high zone.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 4% position in Caterpillar following Evercore's bullish $1,103 price target and its growing appeal as a power infrastructure play.
recent_trades.reasoning: Caterpillar presents a compelling fundamental setup as the market increasingly recognizes its transition from a traditional machinery stock to a critical power infrastructure beneficiary. The recent high-conviction price target upgrade provides an attractive entry point to capture further upside.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing Qualcomm to an 8% weight to capture a predicted massive breakout and raised analyst price targets in the AI semiconductor space.
recent_trades.reasoning: Qualcomm has shown outstanding relative strength, boasting a 5.23% gain over the last 30 days. Highly positive sentiment from recent analyst notes, including Melius Research citing more upside in AI edge computing, justifies expanding our footprint to 8%.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Oracle to a 5% position to free up capital for stronger momentum opportunities in semiconductors and industrials.
recent_trades.reasoning: While Oracle remains a solid AI infrastructure play, its recent 30-day performance (-1.09%) has been lackluster compared to peers. Reducing this position from 10% to 5% allows us to harvest cash and reallocate capital toward stocks with immediate positive catalysts and superior technical setups.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming META again as legal-risk headlines keep capping upside and the stock is fading intraday, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: META sentiment remains negative on lawsuit-settlement risk, and the intraday chart is soft versus peers after a recent failed push higher. We already reduced the name earlier, and with only a 2% weight left this is a risk-management trim to 1% rather than a full exit.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a small CAT position on bullish analyst support and a breakout toward 30-day highs, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: CAT is up on the day, has strong 5D and 30D performance, and is trading just below its 30-day high after constructive intraday candles. Positive news flow around power infrastructure exposure and an Evercore price-target increase supports momentum, so a starter 3% weight is appropriate without overcommitting near highs.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 1.25% position in LMT on positive defense catalysts and price recovery signal, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: LMT benefits from multiple positive drivers: Alabama expansion, AUKUS submarine win, and a 'time to revisit' narrative after recent weakness. The stock is showing signs of stabilization, and defense stocks offer portfolio diversification against tech-heavy holdings.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming GOOGL to 1.0% amid no positive sentiment in news flow, confidence 5/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: GOOGL lacks positive headlines in the current cycle, and with shares near flat, this small reduction from 1.49% to 1.0% frees up cash for higher-conviction opportunities while maintaining exposure.

recent_trades.summary: Reducing MA to 1.5% on negative news regarding Brazil's payment processors and regulatory scrutiny in Europe, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: MA faces headwinds from news about asking Brazilian processors to pay for Banco Master's failure and potential regulatory moves in the UK and EU. The stock is slightly underperforming with no immediate positive catalyst, so trimming from 1.98% to 1.5% reduces risk.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing HON on its +1.6% intraday breakout, positive sentiment, and NHL partnership news, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: HON is a top performer today with green candles breaking above recent highs; sentiment +0.15 and industrials resilience provide a defensive growth tilt. Lifting from 4.23% to 6% improves balance without exceeding concentration rules, especially as we trim weaker names like NVDA.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to CRM on strong +0.27 sentiment, Wedbush notes on demand and backlog strength, and AI platform momentum, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Current 2.77% weight leaves room to scale; positive headlines contrast with broader tech softness, and recent green candles suggest building momentum. Targeting 5% final weight keeps us well under single-name limits while aligning with the positive news catalyst not yet fully priced in.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming NVDA to reduce its 9.7% portfolio weight on slight intraday weakness and neutral news flow, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Technicals show -0.2% today with a red candle near the middle of its 30D range; news is neutral with AI focus shifting to Dell and others. Reducing concentration from 9.71% to 7% frees capital and risk budget while we retain core exposure through other chip names like QCOM and AMD.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a small BLK position given very positive sentiment (+0.50) on tokenization growth narrative and its inclusion in top dividend growth portfolio recommendations.
recent_trades.reasoning: BLK has very positive sentiment (+0.50) driven by Standard Chartered's $4T tokenization forecast (BLK is well-positioned as a leading asset manager to benefit), plus positive dividend growth coverage. The stock is virtually flat on the day and near its 30-day range midpoint, offering a reasonable entry. A 5% weight is conservative given BLK's stable fundamentals and the emerging tokenization tailwind.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to LLY on a third Phase III win for its next-generation obesity drug and record weight-loss results for its GLP-1 pill Foundayo — a strong new fundamental catalyst.
recent_trades.reasoning: Three separate LLY headlines today show: a third Phase III win for next-gen obesity drug, the GLP-1 pill Foundayo showing 'significant' weight loss in older adults, and a new triple agonist setting weight-loss records. This is a materially positive catalyst cluster beyond what I bought on previously. Current weight is 7.11%, and increasing to 9% reflects high conviction in the GLP-1 growth story with concrete Phase III evidence.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting MA as negative sentiment (-0.23) from UK/Europe regulatory proposals and Brazil payment processor dispute create meaningful headwinds with the position already slightly underwater.
recent_trades.reasoning: MA faces a triple regulatory threat: UK PSR new reporting rules, European moves scrutinizing payment networks, and Brazil asking processors to help cover a bank failure. Sentiment is -0.23 (negative). The position is -0.27% below cost with no clear near-term catalyst to reverse. Better to redeploy capital into higher-conviction ideas.

recent_trades.summary: Doubling the TJX position to a 10% weight to capitalize on its Q1 earnings beat and raised full-year guidance.
recent_trades.reasoning: TJX is demonstrating broad-based strength and market share gains, reflected in its highly positive sentiment (+0.26) and raised outlook. I am using the proceeds from the Meta sale to increase my exposure to this confirmed retail winner.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting Meta completely to avoid the headline risk of a looming $400 billion school lawsuit and to free up capital for better opportunities.
recent_trades.reasoning: Meta's sentiment has turned negative (-0.19) following news of significant legal liabilities regarding social media use in schools. With my cash balance at a low 2.1%, liquidating this 4.95% position provides necessary capital to allocate toward companies with clear fundamental tailwinds.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating small CAT position on Evercore upgrade to $1,103 price target and infrastructure/power narrative, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Evercore raised CAT target to $1,103 (current ~$889), citing power infrastructure angle. Stock has +0.75% 5-day momentum and is near 30-day highs. Defensive industrial with catalyst. Starting small at 4%.

recent_trades.summary: Adding slightly to TJX after Q1 earnings beat and full-year guidance raise, broad-based strength and market share gains, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: TJX beat Q1 expectations and raised full-year guidance. Headlines cite broad-based strength, market share gains, and international expansion. Stock is down -0.6% today despite the beat, suggesting a potential buy-the-dip opportunity. Currently 5.06%, nudging to 6%.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating QCOM position on analyst price target raises and +5% 5-day momentum breakout, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Melius Research raised QCOM's price target citing AI semiconductor upside. Stock has +5% 5-day momentum, the strongest in the universe. Prediction articles say analysts see 'massive breakout.' Initiating at 4% — conservative given extended move but momentum is strong.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to LLY on third Phase III win for next-gen obesity drug and triple agonist setting weight-loss records — major fundamental catalyst, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: LLY just reported its third Phase III win for its next-gen obesity drug, and the triple agonist is setting weight-loss records vs Novo. This is a clear fundamental catalyst. Stock is up only 0.2% today, suggesting the market hasn't fully priced this in yet. Currently at 10.1% weight, adding to 13%.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming META as fresh legal-risk headlines weaken the near-term risk-reward despite decent price resilience, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: META sentiment has turned negative on the settlement and school-lawsuit overhang, and the stock is sitting near the upper end of its recent range without a clear new upside catalyst. Since it was only recently increased, this is a moderate trim rather than a full exit, freeing capital for cleaner setups while retaining some exposure.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to TJX after its earnings beat and raised guidance support a stronger breakout setup, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: TJX has the strongest same-day company-specific catalyst in the portfolio with positive sentiment, a guidance raise, and market-share gain commentary. Technically the stock is stabilizing just above the low end of its 30-day range after the headline-driven move, and the current 5.56% weight leaves room to scale it toward a medium-sized position without overconcentration.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing Pfizer to 2% on positive news regarding undervaluation, reduced patent risks, and accretive M&A efforts, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: PFE is underweight at ~1% and showing signs of being undervalued per recent analysis. Positive sentiment from reduced regulatory risks and strategic M&A. Adding to a more meaningful allocation while maintaining diversification within healthcare. Cash available covers the increase.

recent_trades.summary: Expanding Intuitive Surgical to an 8% position following positive analyst commentary on the Da Vinci 5 upgrades deepening its competitive moat.
recent_trades.reasoning: The rollout of the Da Vinci 5 system continues to attract positive coverage, specifically regarding its ability to deepen ISRG's economic moat and pricing power. With a positive sentiment backdrop and steady technicals, the stock is well-positioned for a larger portfolio allocation.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing AMD to an 8% weight to capitalize on a fresh $10 billion news catalyst and positive intraday price action.
recent_trades.reasoning: A recent headline indicating a major $10 billion development for AMD has sparked a midday rally and revived buyer interest. Increasing the position size captures this newly injected momentum and strengthens our semiconductor exposure.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Eli Lilly to an 8% position due to notably negative sentiment scores despite recent clinical data releases, defensively freeing up capital.
recent_trades.reasoning: Although the headlines regarding weight-loss and cancer prevention benefits appear positive on the surface, the heavily negative sentiment score (-0.41) suggests underlying concerns, side effects, or a 'sell the news' market reaction. Trimming exposure from 12% to 8% locks in gains and frees up capital for better-aligned opportunities.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming CVX due to negative sentiment from political headlines and -0.6% intraday performance.
recent_trades.reasoning: CVX sentiment at -0.09 with news of state warnings and mixed commodity commentary; it lags EOG which has very positive +0.51 sentiment and upgrades. Reducing from 1.99% to 1% weight to free cash, limit energy concentration, and avoid repeating prior energy buys.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to AMD on its +1% rally, $10B investor news, and green candlestick momentum.
recent_trades.reasoning: AMD shows neutral sentiment but positive headlines around investor gains and S&P session leadership; technicals indicate breakout from recent range with higher highs. Increasing from 2.11% to 4% weight as AI chip demand remains a core theme without repeating any same-ticker actions from recent runs.

universe.description: 79-name universe: 75 top U.S. mega/large-cap stocks with full GICS sector coverage plus 4 commodity ETFs (gold, silver, oil, copper).

claude._pin_status: UNPINNED: Anthropic echoes the alias unchanged; current dated snapshot id unconfirmed. Resolve manually before pinning (a wrong id silently changes the running model).

gemini._pin_status: UNPINNED: Google echoes the alias unchanged. The adapter now captures response.model_version, so the real dated id will surface in model_id_returned on the next live run; pin from that.

deepseek._pin_note: Deliberately repointed deepseek-v4-flash -> deepseek-v4-pro on 2026-05-21: the deepseek-reasoner alias deprecates 2026-07-24 and maps to sub-frontier v4-flash; v4-pro is DeepSeek's current frontier reasoning tier, restoring cohort parity. No dated immutable snapshot exists beneath deepseek-v4-pro, so this is the most specific callable id (same standard as the dateless Claude ids); residual drift risk if DeepSeek repoints it. Runs in thinking mode at reasoning_effort=high (see deepseek_adapter.py).

mvp_trade.summary: Trimming QCOM to 2.5% to capture a 15.8% gain and reallocate to new high-conviction ideas, confidence 6/10.

market_brief.brief: Welcome. U.S. equities: S&P +0.68%, Nasdaq +0.61%, Dow +0.89%. Technology led sectors (+2.29%) while Commodities lagged (-0.46%). GPT-5.4 led the day at +0.16%. 3 of 6 models green on the day. Cumulative leader: DeepSeek Reasoner at +22.03%. Key headline: Oil prices fall more than 6% as Trump says Iran negotiations in final stages - Fox News — market reaction muted.
market_brief.key_moves: QCOM +11.5% (held by 5/6) | MRK +5.2% (held by 1/6) | AMD +4.8% (held by 4/6)
market_brief.macro_sentence: Key headline: Oil prices fall more than 6% as Trump says Iran negotiations in final stages - Fox News — market reaction muted.
